senses.definition: used to imply that sometimes it is wiser to remain silent than to speak, as it can prevent one from saying something unnecessary or hurtful and allow one to listen, reflect, and learn from others

word_list.word: beware of a silent man and still water

senses.definition: used to imply that people who do not speak much or show their emotions can sometimes have hidden motives or qualities, and one should be cautious and attentive around them

senses.definition: used to suggest that when someone remains silent in a situation where they could speak out or object, it can be interpreted as a form of agreement or acceptance

word_list.word: speech is silver, (but|) silence is golden
word_list.pronunciation: sp藞i藧t蕛 瑟z s藞瑟lv蓺 b藢蕦t 蓴藧晒 s藞a瑟l蓹ns 瑟z 伞藞o蕣ld蓹n

senses.definition: used to suggest that sometimes it is better to remain quiet and not speak out, as silence can be more valuable and effective than speaking

word_list.word: a still tongue makes (for|) a wise head
word_list.pronunciation: 蓯 st藞瑟l t藞蕦艐 m藢e瑟ks f蓴藧晒 蓴藧晒 蓯 w藞a瑟z h藞蓻d

senses.definition: used to imply that it is often wise to remain quiet and listen carefully before speaking or taking action

word_list.word: silence is the best response to a fool
word_list.pronunciation: s藞a瑟l蓹ns 瑟z 冒蓹 b藞蓻st 晒瑟sp藞蓱藧ns t蕣 蓯 f藞u藧l

senses.definition: used to suggest that it is often wiser to remain silent and refrain from engaging in arguments or discussions with foolish, ignorant, or irrational individuals

examples.x: When his coworker started making ridiculous claims at the meeting, John remembered that silence is the best response to a fool, and decided not to engage in an argument.
